does: This paper introduces Reinforcement Learning Guidance (RLG), an inference-time control method for aligning diffusion models with downstream objectives without requiring model retraining. The approach reinterprets RL fine-tuning for diffusion models through stochastic differential equations and implicit reward conditioning, then adapts Classifier-Free Guidance by combining outputs of base and RL fine-tuned models via geometric averaging. The method enables users to adjust alignment strength post-training through a guidance scale parameter, with theoretical analysis showing this scale is mathematically equivalent to modulating KL-regularization strength in the underlying reward objective. Experiments demonstrate RLG effectively balances sample quality with alignment to diverse objectives including human preferences and compositional accuracy while maintaining computational efficiency at inference time.
connects: This work builds on the recent wave of RL fine-tuning methods adapted from RLHF successes in large language models to diffusion models, addressing limitations in existing approaches like reward-weighted diffusion and RL-based fine-tuning that lack post-hoc control. The paper extends Classifier-Free Guidance, a foundational technique in conditional generation, by reframing it through the lens of reward conditioning and stochastic differential equations. It connects to broader research on controlling generative model outputs through guidance mechanisms and the growing literature on aligning diffusion models with human preferences and auxiliary objectives. The theoretical framework builds on implicit reward modeling and the mathematical foundations of diffusion processes as continuous stochastic systems.
enables: This work opens several directions for future research on controllable generation. The inference-time adjustment mechanism enables real-time trade-offs between generation quality and alignment strength without retraining, supporting applications requiring dynamic preference specification. The theoretical connection between guidance scales and reward regularization could inform principled approaches to multi-objective guidance and compositional control in diffusion models. The method's separation of base model and reward-guided model suggests potential for combining multiple aligned models or objectives simultaneously, and the framework could extend to other generative paradigms including flow matching and latent diffusion models, enabling practical deployment of aligned generative systems with flexible post-hoc control.
